title: saxifraga lyallii subsp. hultenii
intro:
Saxifraga lyallii subsp. hultenii is a rare, alpine species of flowering plant that is native to the mountainous regions of Alaska and Canada, characterized by its white flowers and small, rounded leaves.

It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.

The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and acting as an expectorant to relieve respiratory congestion.

In herbal preparations, Saxifraga lyallii subsp. hultenii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to soothe sore throats, calm coughs, and promote overall well-being.
